en: Let's learn how to play the English opening with c4 in just 60 seconds.
So after e5 we go knight c3, knight f6, knight f3, knight c6, and after g3 we can get a nice
reverse dragon with d5, take, take, bishop g2, knight b6, and there are a lot of good
I like the idea of d3, bishop b3, a3, b4, rook c1, and getting knight c5 as a good middle
If I don't play d5 I just tend to get a very comfortable position where I can often use
that d5 outpost for the knight, which is quite nice.
Of course there are a lot of things I can play against the English, like symmetrical
English is another line, but you can do the same kind of thing like knight c3, g3, bishop
g2, knight f3, and d4 tends to work quite well if they let you play it, otherwise if
they don't let you play d4, well then the plan of going a3, rook b1, b4, and chipping
away at the queenside works pretty well.
If knight f6 you go knight c3 and kind of see what they do here, and if c6 you can always
go knight f3 and still play in the Catalan Slav spirit without the move d4.
See you guys in the next video.
